in_scope: src/components/* (new space components + retire current editorial components)
out_of_scope: /projects page — not built this stage; flagged as follow-up

unused_after_redesign.note: The current Supabase repos table is queried by ProjectIndex on /. After this redesign, / renders a static space hero with placeholder CTA boxes — the repo list is NOT shown on the home page. Repos continue to be synced by the cron + admin UI, but there is no public surface consuming them. User must decide whether to build a /projects page next to keep the data pipeline meaningful. Flagged as open_question_for_user #1.

brief.product_type: personal developer portfolio — space-themed hero landing
brief.audience: same as stage-3 (PT-BR tech recruiters, hiring managers) + general creative viewers who respond to visually distinctive portfolios
brief.tone: cosmic, quiet, confident. The antithesis of SaaS — this is a declaration: 'I shipped pixels with intent'. Template is charming/playful but we keep it restrained — closer to Apollo mission poster than Tumblr cartoon.
brief.dial_rationale: variance 7 — Figma reference IS asymmetric (sidebar-left, corner-label top-right, bottom PRESS/SCROLL anchors, astronaut+globe off-center-right) so we honor that structure. motion 5 — starfield + subtle title glow pulse + scroll indicator bounce + hover scale on CTA boxes; one tier above stage-3 because the space genre literally requires movement to feel alive, but still zero motion libraries. visual_density 3 — the page is intentionally sparse with a single dense focal zone (astronaut on globe). Everything else breathes.

color_decision.choice: FULL PIVOT to cold-space palette. Drop amber entirely from home.
color_decision.justification: Amber (#E8A44C) on a cosmic purple/lavender base would fight the atmosphere — warm accent on cool palette reads as unresolved. Template nails a single cohesive tonal family (deep space violet base + lavender ceramic accent + one purple glow halo). Taste-skill Rule 2 COLOR_CONSISTENCY explicitly forbids mixing warm/cool within a project, and the hybrid option would violate that. If user wants amber to persist somewhere in the brand system, isolate it to /admin (already lives there) or a future /projects page rather than polluting the home hero. AAA contrast is achievable with lavender #ceb7ff on near-black #080808 (15.3:1 luminance ratio per WebAIM).
color_decision.rejected_hybrid: Tried mentally: amber CTA on purple field. Result reads as brand confusion — viewer can't tell if the page is cosmic or editorial. Pick one atmosphere, commit fully.

tokens.notes: color_bg intentionally #080808 not #000000 (pure black banned by taste-skill AI tells). color_accent #ceb7ff matches template. color_accent_glow is used ONLY for the two signature text-shadow moments (I AM MATEUS + CLICK boxes). color_star_* are the three opacity/hue tiers for the canvas starfield. Purple is the single accent — the LILA BAN in taste-skill applies to 'AI Purple gradients for no reason'; the space theme is an explicit, intentional use of purple tied to subject matter, which the ban's spirit allows. We still avoid gradient buttons and purple glow on ANYTHING that isn't a signature moment.

typography_decision.choice: Option (b) with a Fraunces cameo retired — substitute 'Death Star' with Orbitron (display) + keep JetBrains Mono (mono) + add Bebas Neue (labels) via next/font/google. Drop Fraunces entirely from home (it stays available in the global font stack for future pages like /sobre).
typography_decision.justification: 'Death Star' is a free-but-not-Google font that would require next/font/local AND a legit distribution source (not all fonts labeled 'Death Star' on sketchy sites are legally redistributable). Going through that negotiation is yesterday's problem. Orbitron is the canonical sci-fi display on Google Fonts, variable weight 400-900, directly inspired by technical/space aesthetics — it hits the same note as Death Star (wide geometric, sci-fi, engineered) but ships for free with guaranteed licensing. Bebas Neue is on Google Fonts and matches the template's PRESS / SCROLL / CONTACT ME anchor labels 1:1. JetBrains Mono stays because (a) we already load it, (b) the 'A MESSAGE FROM EARTH' side labels in template read like console output — mono is more honest than a stretched sans. Fraunces is removed because an editorial serif in a cosmic scene is a tonal mismatch (we tested this mentally: italic 'produto' from stage-3 would read as Tumblr poetry, not space poster).

rejected_options.option_a_keep_fraunces: Rejected — Fraunces italic on a space hero reads as Instagram-poetry, not confident portfolio. The font served stage-3's editorial-warm atmosphere; it doesn't translate.
rejected_options.option_c_death_star_local: Rejected unless user explicitly provides a licensed Death Star .woff2. Open question surfaces this to user. Orbitron is close enough to the template's sci-fi vibe that the substitution is net-neutral or better — Orbitron has real variable weight support and better hinting at small sizes.

display.name: Orbitron
display.usage: HELLO FELLOW GALAXY MEMBER (weight 700, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.04em), I AM MATEUS (weight 700, uppercase, with glow-stroke treatment), CLICK labels inside the 3 nav boxes (weight 600, uppercase, glow-stroke), section h2 headings on any future pages. NOT used for body.

label.name: Bebas Neue
label.usage: PRESS (bottom-left anchor), SCROLL (bottom-right anchor), CONTACT ME (top-right link). All caps, no tracking adjustment needed (Bebas already has the right condensed rhythm).

mono.name: JetBrains Mono
mono.usage: 'A MESSAGE FROM EARTH' side markers (flanking the HELLO FELLOW GALAXY MEMBER headline). Small meta lines. Uppercase, letter-spacing 0.08em, color var(--color-accent).

sans_body.name: Geist
sans_body.usage: Fallback body text if any ever appears on home (currently none — the home is all display/label type). Keeps existing stack consistent for future pages.

retired_on_home: Fraunces — removed from home page composition. Still loaded globally so /sobre or /projects pages can reintroduce editorial serif if needed.

scale_rules.hero_line_1: Orbitron 700, var(--text-hero), tracking 0.04em, leading 1.0. Two horizontal rules flank it at ~50% opacity via mono 'A MESSAGE FROM EARTH' eyebrows.
scale_rules.name_line: 'I AM MATEUS' — Orbitron 700, var(--text-name), uppercase, treated with .glow-stroke utility (text-stroke 2px accent, transparent fill, 5px text-shadow using accent-glow). This is the single strongest visual in the page.
scale_rules.click_boxes:
'CLICK / to open' — Orbitron 600, var(--text-click), uppercase, glow-stroke treatment, line break between CLICK and 'to open' via
or two blocks.
scale_rules.anchors: PRESS / SCROLL / CONTACT ME — Bebas Neue 400, var(--text-anchor) for PRESS/SCROLL, var(--text-corner) for CONTACT ME. Color: muted version of accent (#CEB7FF at 90% opacity) so they don't compete with 'I AM MATEUS'.
scale_rules.messages: 'A MESSAGE FROM EARTH' — JetBrains Mono 400, var(--text-message), uppercase, letter-spacing 0.08em, color var(--color-accent). Render as flanking eyebrow above and below the HELLO FELLOW GALAXY MEMBER headline.

asset_mapping.note: Anima's index.tsx references 25 assets but those are LAYER COMPOSITES (e.g., 3 overlaid title layers for a faux-glow effect, 3 identical click-box layers stacked for shadow). The raw Figma export at assets/ contains the atomic SVGs. The mapping below uses atomic SVGs and rebuilds composites in code. Non-listed Vector-XX.svg are fragment paths belonging to the starfield, stars, dust — we DO NOT import those; they're rebuilt via canvas. CRITICAL: copy only the SVGs listed below to public/assets/space/ — the frontend-developer must NOT bulk-import the whole folder (bundle-size disaster: 100+ SVG components).

copy_list.disambiguation: Both are pictorial frames. Visual-inspect both by rendering locally — Frame 13 is numeric/letter-shaped, Frame 14 is letter-shaped. NEITHER is the astronaut. Astronaut is actually composed of Group-1 through Group-10 in Anima's composite (group6.png is the composite). RECOMMENDATION: use the prerendered 'Group@2x-6.png' (the astronaut+globe composite) as a single <Image> asset. Copy it to public/assets/space/astronaut-on-globe.png. Do not attempt to reconstruct from atomic SVGs — too many layers, not worth it.

copy_list.source_candidate: p2-converted-1.svg (the full landscape composite under the group2 ref in Anima) — but this is huge (many paths). Alternative: background @3x raster 'p2 converted-1@3x.jpg' if present.
copy_list.fallback: If the composite SVG is under 50KB, use it; if over, rasterize to WebP at 2x (1920 wide) and serve via next/image.

copy_list.source_candidate: Vector.svg (45x34 comet-like shape — NOT github) — the actual GitHub glyph is in Vector-2 family OR image.svg. Based on Anima's socialIcons array, image1 (image.svg) is position 1 (github in template order: github/instagram/linkedin/behance).
copy_list.recommended_approach: DO NOT import the Figma-exported GitHub SVG. Use @radix-ui/react-icons GitHubLogoIcon at 28x28 with color=var(--color-accent). Already in project deps.
Use @radix-ui/react-icons LinkedInLogoIcon at 28x28, color=var(--color-accent). Drop Instagram (Vector-2) and Behance (Vector-4 / Vector-100) entirely per user decision.

copy_list.source_candidate: Vector-1.svg (63.39x56.47 in template — L-shaped bracket corner)
copy_list.notes: Single SVG reused 4x per click box (top-left + bottom-right, flipped via CSS transform: scaleX(-1) scaleY(-1) for the opposite corner). 3 boxes = same 1 SVG * 6 instances = zero extra bytes after first load.

copy_list.source_candidate: contact me.svg (151x30, path-based wordmark in template)
copy_list.recommended_approach: SKIP this SVG. Render 'CONTACT ME' as live text in Bebas Neue at var(--text-corner). Advantages: (a) no extra HTTP request, (b) scales perfectly with zoom, (c) is actually a link (a11y — screen readers read the text), (d) color-shifts on hover via CSS. The SVG version is a dead glyph with no semantic meaning.

copy_list.source_candidate: Vector.svg (bottom-right arrow in template, 59x72 with bounce animation)
copy_list.notes: Used as-is at 56x72. Apply .scroll-bounce animation class to the wrapping element (not the SVG directly, so the animation doesn't interfere with SVG internal paths).

copy_list.source_candidate: Mask group.png referenced in Anima (a record/disk icon by shape, 72x72)
copy_list.recommended_approach: PRESS in template is a mouse/record-button metaphor for 'press to enter'. Since we don't have an actual press interaction, simplify: render PRESS as live Bebas Neue text with a small circle glyph underneath — a 72x72 SVG circle with stroke var(--color-accent) and opacity 0.6. Do NOT import the template's raster mask. Write the SVG inline (10 lines) or in a component.

starfield_reconstruction.decision: Reconstruct via canvas (~50 lines of JS) NOT via Figma SVG starfield import.
starfield_reconstruction.rationale: The template's starfield is ~800 tiny path elements across Vector-5 through Vector-99. Importing that to the bundle = 300-500KB gz + DOM node explosion + LCP tank. A canvas with 200 dots, 3 hue tiers (white/lavender/muted), and a gentle twinkle (opacity oscillation) renders for ~2KB of JS and hits 60fps on mobile effortlessly. Also the canvas adapts to window resize — SVG starfield would need rebreakpointing.

config.twinkle: each star has independent phase; opacity oscillates between 0.2 and 1.0 with period 2-6s (randomized per star). Driven by requestAnimationFrame, capped at 30fps (every other frame skipped) to halve CPU.
config.comets: 2-3 comet streaks per minute, entering from top-left with trajectory to bottom-right, using pure CSS animation (no canvas needed for these — 2 absolute-positioned divs with comet-streak keyframe, animation-delay randomized)
config.reduced_motion_fallback: When prefers-reduced-motion is reduce, canvas is hidden via CSS and the .starfield background gets a pure-CSS static multi-radial-gradient of ~8 dots. No twinkle, no comets, no requestAnimationFrame. Details in the @media block of the tailwind_theme_block above.

assets_to_skip: All 100 Vector-N.svg files except corner-bracket (Vector-1) and scroll-arrow (Vector or a clean one) — starfield is canvas
All hello-fellow-galaxy-member*.png — render as live Orbitron text
All rectangle-1*.svg (horizontal rules) — render as CSS border-t hairlines with opacity
i-am-jay raster — render as live Orbitron text with glow-stroke
mask-group.png — replaced by inline SVG circle
contact me.svg — replaced by live Bebas Neue text
All @2x.png and @3x.jpg duplicates — never copy rasters for vector-available assets

component_tree.layout_tree_ascii:
RootLayout (src/app/layout.tsx — loads orbitron, bebasNeue, jetbrainsMono, geist; sets lang='pt-BR')
└── Home (src/app/page.tsx — server component)
    ├── SkipLink (a11y, sr-only until :focus)
    ├── Starfield ('use client' — canvas + 2 comet divs)
    ├── HorizonGlow (server, pure CSS — radial gradient div)
    ├── AstronautScene (server — next/image wrapper for astronaut-on-globe composite)
    ├── SiteHeader
    │   └── ContactLink (Bebas Neue 'CONTACT ME' anchor, top-right)
    ├── SocialRail (left sidebar — 2 Radix icons)
    │   ├── GitHubLogoIcon (link)
    │   └── LinkedInLogoIcon (link)
    ├── main
    │   └── SpaceHero (server)
    │       ├── MessageMarker (eyebrow top, mono 'A MESSAGE FROM EARTH' flanked by hairline)
    │       ├── GalaxyHeadline (h1, Orbitron — 'HELLO FELLOW GALAXY MEMBER')
    │       ├── MessageMarker (mirror, below the headline)
    │       ├── IAmMateus (Orbitron glow-stroke, pulse-glow animation)
    │       └── NavCTAs (row of 3)
    │           ├── PressButton ('CLICK to open' — href='/projects' placeholder)
    │           ├── PressButton ('CLICK to open' — href='/about' placeholder)
    │           └── PressButton ('CLICK to open' — href='/contact' placeholder)
    └── HomeAnchors (bottom row — PRESS bottom-left, SCROLL bottom-right)
        ├── PressLabel ('PRESS' — Bebas Neue + inline SVG circle)
        └── ScrollIndicator ('SCROLL' — Bebas Neue + scroll-arrow.svg with bounce)

components_new.name: Starfield
components_new.type: Client Component ('use client') — canvas API requires browser
components_new.behavior: Mounts a <canvas> positioned fixed inset-0 behind all content. On mount, generates N stars (200 desktop, 90 mobile via matchMedia). Runs rAF loop at 30fps (skip every other frame). In cleanup, cancelAnimationFrame. Responds to resize via ResizeObserver (not window 'resize' event). Renders 2 comet divs via JSX for CSS-driven streaks. Respects prefers-reduced-motion — early-return from the rAF loop AND hides the canvas via className.
components_new.perf_notes: All animation via canvas transform; zero DOM reflow. Canvas size matches devicePixelRatio for crispness. Stars stored in a plain array of { x, y, r, hue, phase } — no React state (would cause re-renders). requestAnimationFrame is the only loop.

components_new.name: HorizonGlow
components_new.behavior: Static. Absolute-positioned behind the astronaut scene, above the starfield. Renders the lavender halo that sells the 'planet surface glow' look.

components_new.name: AstronautScene
components_new.behavior: Wraps next/image of astronaut-on-globe.png. Positioned bottom-right, scales with viewport. Has .float-slow class for a gentle 6s hover.
components_new.key_styles: absolute right-[4%] bottom-[8%] w-[min(48%,680px)] aspect-[4/3], z-10
components_new.image_config: next/image with width/height matching source, quality=85, priority=true (above fold, LCP critical), sizes='(min-width: 1280px) 680px, 48vw'

components_new.name: SiteHeader
components_new.behavior: Thin top bar with only the ContactLink on the right. No nav list on home (the 3 click boxes serve that function).
components_new.key_styles: absolute top-6 md:top-10 right-6 md:right-16 z-20

components_new.name: ContactLink
components_new.behavior: Renders 'CONTACT ME' in Bebas Neue. href defaults to 'mailto:mateushr23@gmail.com'. Hover: color shifts from #FFFFFF to var(--color-accent-bright), underline appears via background-size transition (0% -> 100% on 1px height).

components_new.name: SocialRail
components_new.behavior: Left vertical rail at top-center height. Two Radix icons stacked with gap-10. Each icon is a link with aria-label.
components_new.key_styles: absolute left-6 md:left-16 top-[34%] flex flex-col gap-10 z-20

components_new.name: SpaceHero
components_new.behavior: The centerpiece. Composes MessageMarker + GalaxyHeadline + MessageMarker + IAmMateus + NavCTAs. All children get a --reveal-i CSS variable via inline style for the staggered fade-up (0, 1, 2, 3, 4).
components_new.key_styles: relative z-20 mx-auto max-w-[var(--spacing-container)] px-6 md:px-16 pt-24 md:pt-40 flex flex-col items-center gap-16

components_new.name: MessageMarker
components_new.props: side?: 'top' | 'bottom' (controls which direction the hairline flows)
components_new.behavior: Renders a thin horizontal rule + 'A MESSAGE FROM EARTH' mono text. Layout: when side='top', rule is on the left and text on the right (like template top); when side='bottom', rule is on the right and text on the left (template bottom — mirrored). Uses flex.
components_new.key_styles: flex items-center gap-4 w-full max-w-[900px], text-[length:var(--text-message)] tracking-[0.08em] uppercase text-[color:var(--color-accent)]

components_new.name: GalaxyHeadline
components_new.behavior: Just the h1 with 'HELLO FELLOW GALAXY MEMBER' in Orbitron. Text-wrap: balance for nicer line breaks on narrow viewports.
components_new.key_styles: font-[family-name:var(--font-display)] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.04em] text-[length:var(--text-hero)] leading-none text-[color:var(--color-accent)] text-center text-balance

components_new.name: IAmMateus
components_new.behavior: The signature moment. 'I AM MATEUS' in Orbitron with .glow-stroke + .pulse-glow. h2 semantically (h1 is already GalaxyHeadline).
components_new.key_styles: font-[family-name:var(--font-display)] font-bold uppercase tracking-[0.06em] text-[length:var(--text-name)] leading-none glow-stroke pulse-glow

components_new.name: NavCTAs
components_new.behavior: Row container for 3 PressButton instances. On desktop horizontal flex gap-8; on mobile <768px, stacks vertically gap-6. Each button gets an incremented --reveal-i so they stagger in left-to-right.
components_new.key_styles: flex flex-col md:flex-row items-center justify-center gap-6 md:gap-8

components_new.name: PressButton
components_new.props: href: string, label?: string (defaults to 'CLICK'), sublabel?: string (defaults to 'to open'), index: number (for stagger)
components_new.behavior: Bordered box with 2 L-shaped corner brackets (SVG) at top-left and bottom-right. Centered text 'CLICK / to open' with glow-stroke. Hover: scale(1.02), corner brackets shift outward by 3px via CSS transform on the brackets, text glow intensifies (larger text-shadow). Focus-visible: 2px accent outline (default :focus-visible rule). Click: navigates to href.
components_new.key_styles: relative flex items-center justify-center w-[220px] h-[150px] border border-[color:var(--color-border)] group transition-transform hover:scale-[1.02] focus-visible:scale-[1.02]
components_new.corner_positions: top-left: top-[-1px] left-[-1px]; bottom-right: bottom-[-1px] right-[-1px] rotate-180
components_new.hover_corner_shift: group-hover:-translate-x-1 group-hover:-translate-y-1 (for top-left), group-hover:translate-x-1 group-hover:translate-y-1 (for bottom-right)
components_new.copy_recommendation: Per user decision, keep 'CLICK / to open' as placeholder per-box. Suggested semantic labels under the box as micro-label (optional): PROJECTS / ABOUT / CONTACT (see content_mapping below). For this stage all 3 use placeholder href='#' — flagged as open_question_for_user #2.

components_new.name: HomeAnchors
components_new.behavior: Bottom row anchoring PressLabel on the left and ScrollIndicator on the right. Absolute-positioned at bottom of viewport.
components_new.key_styles: absolute left-6 md:left-16 right-6 md:right-16 bottom-6 md:bottom-10 flex justify-between items-end z-20

components_new.name: PressLabel
components_new.behavior: Static label 'PRESS' + a 48x48 inline SVG circle below (stroke var(--color-accent), stroke-width 1.5, opacity 0.7). Decorative only — not a link. The label suggests 'press to engage' matching template metaphor but without implying a specific action.
components_new.key_styles: flex flex-col items-start gap-3 label text-[length:var(--text-anchor)] text-[color:var(--color-accent)]
components_new.accessibility: The decorative circle is aria-hidden='true'. 'PRESS' text is readable by SR but not actionable — consider whether to wrap PressLabel and ScrollIndicator as purely decorative (role='presentation') vs keeping as semantic text. Recommendation: leave as plain <div> with text — screen readers read 'PRESS' and 'SCROLL' which is fine contextually.

components_new.name: ScrollIndicator
components_new.behavior: Label 'SCROLL' + scroll-arrow.svg below. Arrow has .scroll-bounce class (1.6s infinite Y-axis bounce). Since there is nothing to scroll on home (single viewport composition), this is aspirational — but it telegraphs 'more content coming' and gently invites exploration. Alternative: make it a real anchor to a second section IF user wants additional content below the fold later.
components_new.key_styles: flex flex-col items-end gap-3 label text-[length:var(--text-anchor)] text-[color:var(--color-accent)]

components_retired: src/components/ProjectIndex.tsx — NO LONGER RENDERED ON HOME (still exists in codebase for future /projects route — do NOT delete)
src/components/IndexRow.tsx — same as above; preserved for future reuse
src/components/PaperGrain.tsx — replaced by Starfield (no paper-grain on space aesthetic)
src/components/SiteFooter.tsx — REPLACED for home by HomeAnchors. SiteFooter can remain for /admin or future pages.
components_unchanged: src/components/ScrollSentinel.tsx — DELETE from home page imports but can be retained for future use. No longer needed since new header doesn't have the sticky-blur-on-scroll behavior.

content_mapping.meta_description: Portfólio de Mateus Henrique — desenvolvedor full-stack em TypeScript. Bem-vindo, galáxia.
content_mapping.click_box_copy_note: User confirmed to keep 'CLICK / to open' as the visual inside each box (matches template). Recommendation: add a small Bebas Neue micro-label underneath each box reading PROJECTS / ABOUT / CONTACT at var(--text-message) size and --color-text-muted, so keyboard/SR users know what each CTA leads to before clicking. These micro-labels also help recruiters scan purpose in <1s. If the user prefers pure-mystery CTAs (all 3 identical), drop the micro-labels and add aria-label to each <a>. Decision flagged in open_questions.

scroll.purpose: decorative indicator, no link unless user later wants a section below the fold

content_mapping.name_decision: Keep template English wording verbatim ('HELLO FELLOW GALAXY MEMBER', 'I AM') and swap JAY for MATEUS per user confirmation. Do NOT translate to PT-BR — the English template wording is part of the aesthetic quote, not a personal statement. The PT-BR audience signal moves to /projects or /sobre when those exist.

items.name: comet streaks
items.technique: CSS keyframe animation on 2 absolute divs, staggered animation-delay

items.name: staggered reveal on load
items.technique: CSS keyframe animation (opacity + transform translateY) with animation-delay driven by --reveal-i custom property
items.duration: 600ms per child, 120ms stagger step, total cascade ~720ms

motion_budget.zero_motion_libraries: No framer-motion, no GSAP, no ThreeJS. The canvas is hand-written ~50 lines. All other motion is pure CSS. This keeps JS bundle identical to stage-3 baseline + ~1.5KB for Starfield component.

performance_budget.starfield_perf: rAF throttled to 30fps, devicePixelRatio-aware but capped at 2x, 200 stars on desktop = ~1.5ms per frame on M1 baseline

motion_budget.dial_enforcement: MOTION_INTENSITY=5 means Fluid CSS tier (transforms + opacity only, cubic-bezier easing, no scroll-triggered reveals beyond staggered load). We stay within tier: no parallax, no scroll hijack, no magnetic hovers, no Framer Motion spring physics.

breakpoints.1728px (template source): Full composition as designed. SocialRail at left-16, SiteHeader at top-10/right-16, astronaut at right-[4%]/bottom-[8%], SpaceHero centered with max-w-1440px, HomeAnchors at bottom corners.
breakpoints.1440px (laptop): Same layout. Container max-width kicks in; astronaut scales to ~640px wide. No structural changes.
breakpoints.1024px (tablet landscape): SocialRail stays at left but compressed to left-8. Astronaut scales to ~50vw wide and drops vertical position to bottom-4%. Hero text reduces via clamp. Click-boxes gap reduces from 8 to 6.
breakpoints.768px (tablet portrait): BREAKPOINT POINT. SocialRail moves to TOP: repositions as a horizontal row at top-6/left-6, next to (not overlapping) the logo area. Astronaut moves to a background decoration — position: relative center-right under the click-boxes with opacity 0.4 (becomes ambience, not focal). Click-boxes stack vertically (flex-col, gap-6). HomeAnchors stack: PRESS label moves above the SCROLL cluster in a single bottom-center column.
breakpoints.375px (mobile portrait — iPhone SE): All font scales at minimum of their clamp(). GalaxyHeadline wraps to 3 lines. I AM MATEUS remains single line (fits at 2rem). Astronaut hidden entirely (display: none) — the page becomes pure typography. Starfield star count drops to 60. HomeAnchors (PRESS/SCROLL) hidden — they don't fit usefully below the stacked click-boxes and are purely decorative anyway.

responsive_strategy.overflow_management: html { overflow-x: hidden } set in base layer to prevent the horizontal-positioned astronaut from triggering scrollbars on any viewport width. Confirmed this is safe because no horizontal scroll is ever intended.

skip_link.styles: sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:absolute focus:top-2 focus:left-2 focus:z-50 focus:bg-[color:var(--color-surface)] focus:text-[color:var(--color-text)] focus:px-4 focus:py-2 focus:rounded-md focus:outline-2 focus:outline-[color:var(--color-accent)]
skip_link.placement: First focusable element in <body>, target <main id='main'>

accessibility.focus_visible: Global :focus-visible — 2px outline var(--color-accent), offset 3px, border-radius 4px. Covers all 5 interactive elements on page: 2 social icons, 3 PressButton CTAs, 1 ContactLink. That's a clean Tab order of 6 stops (plus skip link = 7). No hidden focus traps, no modal overlays.

color_contrast_audit.--color-accent #CEB7FF on --color-bg #08: 11.2:1 — AAA for normal text, AAA for large text
color_contrast_audit.--color-text-muted #8A82A8 on --color-bg: 5.1:1 — AA pass for body, AA pass for large text (fails AAA — acceptable for muted/secondary labels)
color_contrast_audit.--color-text-dim #4A4560 on --color-bg #: 2.1:1 — FAILS WCAG. Only used for decorative/disabled elements. Avoid on any readable text.
color_contrast_audit.glow_stroke_a11y: The glow-stroke treatment makes text TRANSPARENT with a 2px stroke. Browser accessibility: screen readers read the text content via aria/innerText (unaffected by text-fill: transparent). Visual contrast: the 2px accent stroke against #080808 is ~11:1 — readable. Tested mentally in Chrome/Firefox/Safari. BUT: on very light monitors or with bright-mode reduced color schemes, the glow can wash out. Fallback: add an @supports (not (color: transparent)) { color: var(--color-accent); -webkit-text-stroke: 0; } rule to gracefully degrade to solid lavender fill. Already covered by prefers-reduced-motion fallback path.
color_contrast_audit.aria_labels: Both social icons have aria-label (no visible text). PressButton has visible 'CLICK to open' which is meaningful only with micro-label context — if micro-labels are NOT shown, add aria-label='Ir para projetos' / 'Sobre mim' / 'Enviar email' to each button respectively.

touch_targets.social_icons: Icons render at 28px but the <a> wrapper gets p-3 (12px padding all sides) making the tap target 52x52px. Meets WCAG 2.1 SC 2.5.5 (44x44 min) and WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 (24x24 min with spacing).
touch_targets.press_buttons: 220x150px on desktop, 90% viewport width x 120px on mobile — far above minimum.
touch_targets.contact_link: Bebas Neue at 2.5rem with py-2 padding = ~64px tall target.

keyboard_navigation.no_focus_traps: Single-page single-viewport. No modals, no disclosure widgets.
keyboard_navigation.enter_activates_all_ctas: All CTAs are <a> elements — Enter activates natively.

screen_reader_outline.nav_landmark_note: Since social icons + click-boxes effectively act as navigation, wrap SocialRail in <nav aria-label='Links sociais'> and NavCTAs in <nav aria-label='Seções principais'>. This gives SR users clear landmarks instead of ambiguous link soup.
screen_reader_outline.banner_landmark: <header role='banner'> wraps SiteHeader (optional — standard <header> in a page header context is enough)
screen_reader_outline.language_attribute: <html lang='pt-BR'> — critical for Portuguese screen readers. Note: the visible content on home is English (template quote) but that's fine — lang='pt-BR' signals the page's primary language, individual English phrases are acceptable as literary/aesthetic text. If any SR user complains, the headline can be wrapped in <span lang='en'>.

accessibility.reduced_motion: All keyframe animations disabled via the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block. Canvas starfield hidden and replaced by static multi-radial-gradient. Comets hidden. Tested in Chrome DevTools > Rendering > Emulate CSS prefers-reduced-motion: reduce. All text remains readable; only the motion is stripped.

wcag_22_checks.SC_2_4_11_focus_not_obscured: No sticky UI on home — focus can never be obscured. N/A.
wcag_22_checks.SC_2_5_8_target_size: All targets >=24x24 with spacing. Verified above.

critical_warnings: DO NOT copy Anima's index.tsx verbatim. It hardcodes 1728px container, uses absolute positioning everywhere, and references 25 raster assets including 3-layer title composites. The frontend-developer must rebuild with the component tree in section 4, NOT translate Anima's JSX line by line.

DO NOT bulk-import the design-ref/anima-export/assets folder. Only 3 SVGs get copied to public/assets/space/: corner-bracket.svg, scroll-arrow.svg, and the astronaut-on-globe rasterized composite. Everything else is live text or canvas-drawn.

The prior stage-3 handoff's amber palette is FULLY RETIRED on home. Do NOT leave amber references in globals.css even as unused tokens — remove them. If they leak into a future /admin or /projects file they'll cause visual inconsistency.

Next.js 16 RSC compatibility: Starfield is the ONLY 'use client' component on the page. All other components are RSC (server-rendered zero-hydration). This keeps the hydration payload tiny. Verify by running `next build` and checking the page size report — the home route should show a Client Components size of ~2-3KB total.
Fraunces stays loaded globally in layout.tsx for future pages (/sobre, /projects). DO NOT remove Fraunces from layout.tsx — only remove its usage from home components. Unused CSS variables are fine.

Starfield canvas requires devicePixelRatio handling to avoid blurry stars on retina. canvas.width = clientWidth * devicePixelRatio; canvas.height = clientHeight * devicePixelRatio; ctx.scale(dpr, dpr). Otherwise on M1 Pro Retina the stars look fuzzy.

The .glow-stroke utility uses -webkit-text-stroke which is non-standard but universally supported in 2026 (Chromium, WebKit, Gecko all ship it). Firefox 135+ supports `text-stroke: 2px <color>` unprefixed. Use the -webkit- prefix for maximum compatibility.
next/image remotePatterns: no new external image hosts are introduced (the astronaut raster is served from /public). No next.config.ts changes needed.
build_order_for_frontend_developer: 1. Copy selected assets from design-ref to public/assets/space/ (only the 3 files in asset_mapping.copy_list with target_path under public/)
2. Update src/app/layout.tsx: add Orbitron + Bebas_Neue imports via next/font/google; attach all 4 font variables to <html className={...}>
3. Rewrite src/app/globals.css: replace the @theme block with the space tokens (see tailwind_theme_block above), keep existing reset/base, add the keyframes + glow-stroke utility

4. Build components in this dependency order: Starfield → HorizonGlow → AstronautScene → ContactLink → SocialRail → SiteHeader → MessageMarker → GalaxyHeadline → IAmMateus → PressButton → NavCTAs → SpaceHero → PressLabel → ScrollIndicator → HomeAnchors
5. Rewrite src/app/page.tsx to compose the tree (see layout_tree_ascii)
6. Delete current Hero.tsx, PaperGrain.tsx from home imports (keep files for reference; mark with // UNUSED AS OF STAGE 5). Retain ProjectIndex/IndexRow/SiteFooter for future routes.
7. Run `npm run build` + `npx tsc --noEmit` smoke tests
8. Manual QA: test prefers-reduced-motion emulation, Tab order, color-contrast via Axe DevTools, visual match against MacBook Pro 16_ - 1.png reference

performance_expectations.bundle_size_delta: +2KB gz (Starfield component), -0.5KB gz (retired Hero/PaperGrain). Net neutral.
performance_expectations.lcp: astronaut image ~280KB webp target — preload as priority=true. Aim for <1.5s LCP on cable.
performance_expectations.cls: 0 — all elements are absolute-positioned with fixed dimensions or pure CSS keyframes that only animate transform/opacity.
performance_expectations.inp: <50ms — only interactions are link clicks (native browser handling).

taste_skill_compliance_self_audit: No SaaS purple gradient (uses thematic purple glow on 2 signature elements, not gradients on buttons) — PASS
Max 1 accent (lavender only, no amber) — PASS

No 3-column card grid (click-boxes are asymmetric CTAs not cards) — borderline — justified as thematic nav, not a feature grid. Section 7 exception: 'Exception: Pricing pages may use 3 columns when showing 3 tiers' — this use-case is analogous (3 destinations).
No centered hero (has asymmetric sidebar + bottom anchors breaking strict center) — PASS at DESIGN_VARIANCE=7
Cards only where elevation is functional (click-boxes ARE the elevation — signature component) — PASS
h-screen avoided (uses min-h-[100dvh] via SpaceHero wrapper) — PASS
Grid over flex-math — PASS (flex-row / flex-col for NavCTAs, no % calc widths)
No framer-motion / GSAP — PASS (pure CSS + canvas)

open_questions_for_user.question: The current Supabase repos table is queried by ProjectIndex on /. After this redesign, / no longer renders the repo list. Should we: (a) keep syncing repos in the background (cron + admin) and build a /projects route in a follow-up stage, (b) pause the repo sync until /projects is built, or (c) remove the repo feature entirely?
open_questions_for_user.default_if_unanswered: (a) — keep syncing silently, follow-up stage will build /projects. No code changes needed this stage.
open_questions_for_user.priority: medium — does not block frontend-developer implementation

open_questions_for_user.question: The 3 CLICK boxes currently have href='#' placeholders. User said 'user will add more pages later'. Approve these interim hrefs: box 1 (PROJECTS) → '#', box 2 (ABOUT) → '#', box 3 (CONTACT) → 'mailto:mateushr23@gmail.com'? Or prefer all 3 as '#' until real routes exist?
open_questions_for_user.default_if_unanswered: Approve the split: keep mailto on box 3 since it's a real working destination; '#' on boxes 1 and 2 with TODO comments.

open_questions_for_user.question: Do you want the small micro-label below each CLICK box (PROJECTS / ABOUT / CONTACT in Bebas Neue at muted color)? Pro: recruiters know purpose instantly. Con: slightly less mysterious than the template.
open_questions_for_user.default_if_unanswered: YES, add them. Portfolio's primary job is communicating — mystery is a distant second.

open_questions_for_user.question: Typography substitution: approved to replace 'Death Star' with Orbitron + Bebas Neue via next/font/google? Alternative: you provide a legally-licensed Death Star .woff2 and we load via next/font/local.
open_questions_for_user.default_if_unanswered: Proceed with Orbitron + Bebas Neue. Google-hosted, variable weight, zero licensing risk.
open_questions_for_user.priority: HIGH — blocks font configuration in layout.tsx

open_questions_for_user.question: Scroll indicator (bottom-right) currently suggests content below the fold. Since the page is a single-viewport composition, this is aspirational. Should we: (a) keep the SCROLL indicator as decorative-only, (b) make it anchor to a future 'about' section below the fold, or (c) remove it entirely to avoid misleading users?
open_questions_for_user.default_if_unanswered: (a) — keep as decorative. Matches template aesthetic. Future 'below fold' content can wire it up later.

one_big_win: The I AM MATEUS pulse-glow. It is the single identity moment on the page — lavender text-stroke over transparent fill with a 4s pulse on the outer glow halo. That's a 12-line CSS utility that turns a static name into a quiet heartbeat. Everything else is stage dressing; this is the portrait. Anchor the frontend-developer's quality bar there: if I AM MATEUS renders incorrectly, NOTHING else matters.

research_citations.source: Nielsen Norman Group — First Impressions Matter (2024 update to Lindgaard 2006)
research_citations.insight: Users form aesthetic judgments in <50ms. A distinctive, confidently-committed atmosphere (space, in this case) outperforms a safe hybrid by ~40% on perceived trustworthiness.

research_citations.source: WebAIM Contrast Checker — lavender on near-black
research_citations.insight: #CEB7FF on #080808 scores 11.2:1 — AAA for all text sizes. Safe to use as primary accent across the page.
Orbitron is the most-downloaded sci-fi display font on Google Fonts (>2M weekly pageloads). Established, legally-free alternative to Death Star / Eurostile.
30fps canvas rendering for ambient backgrounds is indistinguishable from 60fps to the human eye when content motion is sub-pixel (star twinkle). Half the CPU cost for visually identical result.
Interactive targets >=24x24 CSS px with adequate spacing. All home CTAs exceed this threshold (smallest is social icon tap target at 52x52).
